codeReview.summary: The feature-owned commit cleanly routes Repo::save explicit-id writes through the existing persist_explicit_id_primitive seam already used by create_at and upsert_at. The diff is narrowly scoped to the intended runtime seam, and the focused regressions cited by the worker cover the preserved create-only typed Conflict behavior and shared addressed-row semantics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill does not explicitly document a verification-only path for already-landed features, so workers repeatedly mark followedProcedure=false when the task is artifact/evidence confirmation rather than new implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md requires tests-first implementation steps, while handoff 2026-03-24T07-12-16-613Z__milestone-d-explicit-id-persistence-primitive-save-delegation__220cc1f4-4641-41b2-bf6c-b141e2778955.json records a justified verification-only deviation against already-landed commit c11c0f91128374b4a29818b79a8925b898a2a85a.

summary: Pass: commit c11c0f91128374b4a29818b79a8925b898a2a85a adds the missing Repo::save fast-path so explicit-id save, upsert_at, and create_at now share persist_explicit_id_primitive, while duplicate create_at retains typed Conflict wrapper semantics.
